steps.title: Map packages/contracts
steps.instructions: Author ONE memory note via memory_write: id area-contracts, kind architecture_current, categories [subsystem]. Subject: packages/contracts — the seam layer. Read the source first (fs_list, then fs_read the files you claim about). Title ≤60 chars naming the thing; 2-sentence standalone summary; bullet-fact body ≤1200 chars with exact file paths and function names; links: depends_on for every area this one imports from, chosen ONLY among [area-contracts, area-kernel, area-storage, area-execution, area-memory, area-executor, area-providers, area-cli, area-ui, area-vault], derived from package.json workspace deps and actual imports. End the body with one line: Verified: <files read line-by-line> / skimmed: <files only listed>. Write exactly this one note, modify no repository files, then signal success with a one-line summary naming the note id.

steps.title: Map kernel core
steps.instructions: Author ONE memory note via memory_write: id area-kernel, kind architecture_current, categories [subsystem]. Subject: packages/kernel/src core — kernel.ts task/plan lifecycle, projections.ts fold, plugins/ host+trust+skills, config.ts (EXCLUDE storage/ and execution/ subtrees — sibling steps own those). Same note contract as every area step: read before claiming; title ≤60; 2-sentence summary; bullet-fact body ≤1200 chars with exact paths+functions; depends_on links only among the ten area-* ids, from real imports; end body with 'Verified: <read line-by-line> / skimmed: <listed>'. One note, no repo modifications, signal success with a one-line summary.

steps.title: Assemble hub, cross-cuts, and verify connectivity
steps.instructions: The ten map-* siblings each wrote one area-* note. memory_read ALL of them first. Then author via memory_write: (1) hub note arch-overview (kind architecture_current, categories [subsystem]) — the system in one note (event-sourced multi-agent orchestrator, Postgres event log as the ONLY truth, everything else rebuildable projections), linking decomposes_into EVERY area note; (2) zone-write-fence (kind fact, categories [cross-cutting]) — the fs_write zone mechanism across contracts/kernel/executor/memory, relates_to those four areas; (3) target-memory-lifecycle (kind architecture_target, categories [target]) — decay/sweep/archive designed but deferred, gated on hits data, relates_to area-memory. Then VERIFY: every note reachable from the hub via typed links, ≤14 notes total; if an area note is missing or unreachable, say so explicitly. Signal success with a summary listing areas covered and gaps.
